title: Net worth
description: Practice understanding of net worth: identifying assets and liabilities, calculating net worth, understanding impact on net worth.

assessment_items.question_content:
Knowing what your assets are is important when you're working out your net worth.

**Which of these are assets?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Knowing what your assets are is important when you're working out your net worth.

**Which of these are considered assets?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Knowing what your assets are is important when you're working out your net worth.

**Which of these are assets?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: rent for house you live in
life insurance policy with cash value

assessment_items.question_content:
Before you can calculate your net worth, you must know what your liabilities are. 

**Which of these are liabilities?**

[[☃ radio 2]]

Before you can calculate your net worth, you must know what your liabilities are. 

**Which of these are liabilities?**

[[☃ radio 2]]

Before you can calculate your net worth, you must know what your liabilities are. 

**Which of these are liabilities?**

[[☃ radio 2]]

choices.content: a gift you plan to give

assessment_items.question_content:
Imagine you are looking at the following situation:

Assets | | Liabilities | 
- | -: | -: | -:
Savings | $\$1{,}200$ | Personal loan | $\$1{,}000$
Motorcycle | $\$800$ | Credit card | $\$400$ 
Computer | $\$300$ | Phone bill | $\$200$
**Total assets** | $\$2{,}300$ | **Total liabilities** | $\$1{,}600$

**What is the net worth in this scenario?**

$\$$[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
To calculate net worth we use a formula: 

$\goldE{\text{Net worth}} = \green{\text{Assets}} - \red{\text{Liabilities}}$

We can see that total amount for assets is $\$2{,}300$ and total for liabilities is $\$1{,}600$. 
So, we will plug them into the formula for net worth.

assessment_items.question_content:
Imagine you are looking at the following situation:

Assets | | Liabilities | 
- | -: | -: | -:
used car | $\$2{,}000$ | student loan | $\$1{,}500$
laptop | $\$1{,}000$ | medical bill | $\$500$ 
timeshares | $\$500$ | credit cards | $\$300$
**Total assets** | $\$3{,}500$ | **Total liabilities** | $\$2{,}300$

**What is the net worth in this scenario?**

$\$$[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
To calculate net worth we use a formula: 

$\goldE{\text{Net worth}} = \green{\text{Assets}} - \red{\text{Liabilities}}$

We can see that total amount for assets is $\$3{,}500$ and total for liabilities is $\$2{,}300$. 
So, we will plug them into the formula for net worth.

assessment_items.question_content:
Imagine you are looking at the following situation:

Assets | | Liabilities | 
- | -: | -: | -:
bike | $\$200$ | credit cards | $\$800$
laptop | $\$300$ | student loan | $\$1{,}000$ 
textbooks | $\$100$ | rent | $\$500$
**Total assets** | $\$600$ | **Total liabilities** | $\$2{,}300$

**What is the net worth in this scenario?**

$\$$[[☃ numeric-input 1]]
hints:
To calculate net worth we use a formula: 

$\goldE{\text{Net worth}} = \green{\text{Assets}} - \red{\text{Liabilities}}$

We can see that total amount for liabilities is $\$2{,}300$ and total for assets is $\$600$. 
So, we will plug them into the formula for net worth.

assessment_items.question_content:
Suppose you walk into an electronics store and buy a brand new large-screen TV for $\$1{,}500$ on credit, and you can't pay it all off straight away. 

**How does this scenario affect your net worth?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

You just eliminated your $\$2{,}000$ credit card balance by working some extra jobs. 

**How will paying off this credit card affect your net worth?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Your net worth will go up.
Your net worth will go down.
Your net worth will remain the same.

assessment_items.question_content:
As part of finishing a project, you need to borrow your friends tools. Your friend agrees to loan them to you for the day.

**What will happen to your net worth while you use these tools?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: This action will lower your net worth.
This action will raise your net worth.
This action does not change your net worth.
